content.full_text:
State of California
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. BOX 807 • SACRAMENTO, 95804 • • • 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814

Technical Assistance     Administration     Executive/Legal     Enforcement     Statements of Economic Interest
(916) 322 5660         322 5660             322 5901           322 6441           322 6444

October 9, 1981

Mr. Peter Coye
777 West Seventh Street
Claremont, CA 91711

Dear Mr. Coye:

I am responding to your letter to Mr. Prim of October 2,
1981. You asked whether the Political Reform Act in any way
prohibits you from working for a political committee called
Californians For a Fair Index on time outside your forty
hour work week as an Administrative Assistant for Senator
Watson. I would first like to point out that the Political
Reform Act does not prohibit any political activities.
However, the Act does require that certain activities be
reported. In particular, the Act requires that political
activities by a government employee during government working
hours or with government facilities or materials, be reported
as an in-kind contribution by the government employer. See
2 Cal. Adm. Code Section 18420, a copy of which is enclosed
for your convenience.

According to your letter, you will not be doing any
work for Californians For a Fair Index during your forty
hour work week for the state. In this case, assuming that
no state facilities or materials are used for Californians
For a Fair Index, your activities would not be considered a
contribution by the state to Californians For a Fair Index,
and would not have to be reported under Section 18420.

I hope this answers your question. If you have any
other questions concerning reporting of campaign expenditures
under the Political Reform Act, please feel free to get in
touch with us.

Very truly yours,

Barbara A. Milman
Chief
Local Division

BAM:km
Enclosure

October 2, 1981

Mr. Ted Prim
Executive Director
Fair Political Practices Commission
1100 K Street
P. O. Box 807
Sacramento, CA 95804

Dear Mr. Prim:

I am currently employed as the Administrative Assistant for
State Senator Watson in her District Office. However, as a
private citizen, I am also active in local politics and in
state issues as a volunteer. Needless to say, I want to see
that both Senator Watson and myself are protected from un-
founded charges that my state employment and private interests
are in any way confused or interrelated.

Specifically, I am now donating time outside my 8-hour day
and 10-hour work week for the state as the Treasurer of a
Recipient and Expenditure Committee called "Californians
for a Fair Index." I want to emphasize that all my volunteer
efforts with this group are above and beyond my work with
Senator Watson.

Please inform me if this is appropriate under current FPPC
regulations and acknowledge that you understand my practice
and intent with regard to "Californians for a Fair Index."

Your cooperation in this matter is appreciated.

Sincerely,

Peter Coye

PETER COYE
777 W. 7th St.
Claremont, CA 91711
